questions.question_text: A newly licensed individual wants to list and sell properties independently. Which license type does she need?
distractors: Salesperson license
Associate broker license
Provisional broker license
questions.explanation: Only a broker may operate independently and supervise other licensees in Delaware. Salespersons and associate brokers must work under a licensed broker. The broker license is the highest level and carries full supervisory authority.

questions.question_text: How does an associate broker differ from a salesperson in Delaware?
questions.correct_answer: An AB is broker-qualified but works under another
distractors: An AB may operate an independent office
An AB needs fewer pre-license hours
An AB cannot represent buyers directly
questions.explanation: An associate broker has met all broker qualifications but chooses to work under another broker rather than operating independently. A salesperson is the entry-level license and has not yet met broker requirements. Both must work under a supervising broker.

questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education are required in Delaware before taking the licensing exam?
distractors: 60 hours
120 hours
75 hours
questions.explanation: Delaware requires 99 hours of approved pre-license education before a candidate can sit for the licensing exam. This education covers both national real estate principles and Delaware-specific law and practice.

questions.question_text: A candidate arrives at the testing center with only a driver's license. What will happen?
questions.correct_answer: She will be denied entry to the exam
distractors: She may test with one valid photo ID
She can use a signed affidavit instead
The proctor may waive the second ID
questions.explanation: Delaware requires two forms of identification at the testing center. A single ID, even a valid photo ID like a driver's license, is insufficient. Candidates who do not bring two forms of ID will be turned away and must reschedule.

questions.question_text: A salesperson wants to upgrade to broker status. Which statement is accurate about this process?
questions.correct_answer: She must meet broker qualifications independently
distractors: Her current broker can promote her directly
She only needs additional continuing education
The Commission automatically upgrades after 5 years
questions.explanation: Upgrading from salesperson to broker requires meeting all broker qualifications, including additional education and experience requirements. There is no automatic promotion based on time served, and the supervising broker cannot simply elevate a salesperson's license level.

questions.question_text: A new licensee passes her exam on March 1. By what date must she complete post-license education?
distractors: Within 6 months of initial licensure
Before her first license renewal
Within 30 days of initial licensure
questions.explanation: Delaware requires new licensees to complete 12 hours of post-license education within 90 days of obtaining their initial license. This tight timeline ensures new licensees quickly gain practical knowledge in professional standards, contracts, and professionalism.

questions.question_text: How many hours of post-license education must a new Delaware licensee complete?
distractors: 21 hours over the first renewal period
6 hours in two 3-hour modules
15 hours in five 3-hour modules
questions.explanation: The post-license requirement is 12 hours divided into four 3-hour modules: professional standards, agreement of sale and buyer representation, documents and seller representation, and professionalism. All four modules must be completed within 90 days.

questions.question_text: When do Delaware real estate licenses expire?
distractors: December 31 of odd-numbered years
June 30 of even-numbered years
March 31 of each calendar year
questions.explanation: Delaware real estate licenses operate on a biennial renewal cycle, expiring on April 30 of even-numbered years. Licensees must complete 21 hours of continuing education and submit their renewal application before this date to avoid lapse.

questions.question_text: A broker has completed only 15 of the required continuing education hours before the renewal deadline. What is the total CE requirement?
distractors: 12 hours per two-year renewal cycle
30 hours per two-year renewal cycle
18 hours per two-year renewal cycle
questions.explanation: Delaware requires 21 hours of continuing education every two years for license renewal. Falling short of this requirement by the April 30 deadline in even years could result in the license lapsing or other consequences.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed salesperson has completed only 6 of the required post-license hours within 90 days. What consequence might she face?
questions.correct_answer: Potential disciplinary action by the Commission
distractors: Automatic extension of the 90-day deadline
A small late fee with no further penalty
Transfer to inactive status for six months
questions.explanation: Delaware requires all 12 hours of post-license education within 90 days of licensure. Failure to complete this requirement can result in disciplinary action. New licensees should prioritize completing all four 3-hour modules well before the deadline.

questions.question_text: A salesperson leaves Broker A to join Broker B. What must occur for the transfer to be valid?
questions.correct_answer: The Commission must be notified of the change
distractors: The salesperson must retake the state exam
Broker A must release the license in writing
A 30-day waiting period must be observed
questions.explanation: When a licensee transfers to a new broker, the Commission must be notified. This ensures the Commission's records accurately reflect which broker supervises each salesperson or associate broker. No waiting period or re-examination is required.

questions.question_text: A candidate has failed the Delaware state exam three times. What must she do before attempting the exam again?
distractors: Wait six months before the next attempt
Complete 21 hours of continuing education
Obtain a letter of recommendation from a broker
questions.explanation: Delaware allows a maximum of three attempts to pass the licensing exam. After three failures, the candidate must retake the full pre-license education course before being eligible to sit for the exam again. This policy ensures candidates have adequate preparation.

questions.question_text: A candidate passes the national portion of the exam in January. By when must she pass the state portion?
questions.correct_answer: Within one year of the first passing score
distractors: Within 6 months of the first attempt
Within 90 days of the national portion
There is no time limit between portions
questions.explanation: Delaware requires candidates to pass both exam portions within one year. If the state portion is not passed within one year of passing the national portion, the national score expires and both portions must be retaken.

questions.question_text: A licensee placed her license on inactive status two years ago. What must she do to reactivate?
questions.correct_answer: Meet reactivation requirements set by the Commission
distractors: Simply pay the current renewal fee
Retake and pass both exam portions
Complete only 12 hours of post-license education
questions.explanation: Reactivating an inactive license requires meeting the Commission's reactivation requirements, which may include completing continuing education missed during the inactive period. The specific requirements depend on how long the license has been inactive.

questions.question_text: Which activity requires a Delaware real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: Listing property for sale for compensation
distractors: Selling your own personal residence
Managing your own rental property
Providing mortgage lending services
questions.explanation: Listing, selling, buying, or exchanging real estate for others for compensation requires a license. Selling your own property, managing your own rentals, and mortgage lending fall outside the scope of real estate licensure. The key triggers are acting for others and receiving compensation.
